name: four-dx-coach
description: Multi-scope coach for The 4 Disciplines of Execution — personal solo (agent = peer-witness), team-leader (agent = consultant), team-member (agent = personal coach to inherited-WIG situation). 12 skills with v0.8.0 dual-mode architecture: every topic skill supports both **coach-mode** (Socratic dialogue, fit-check inclusive, walks you step-by-step from zero via `protocols/coach-mode.md`) AND **audit-mode** (single-layer synthesis from one existing artifact at that topic's layer via `protocols/audit-mode.md`). Skill inventory: 1 plugin router (`using-four-dx-coach`) + 10 dual-mode topic skills (5 multi-file scope-flex — meta-strategy-triage, d1-wig-formulation, d2-lead-measures, d3-scoreboard, d4-cadence; 5 single-file scope-specific — meta-whirlwind-triage, d1-wig-cascade, meta-team-leader-onboarding, meta-xps-evaluation, sustain-momentum-rescue) + 1 cross-layer aggregator (`4dx-audit`) reserved for multi-artifact spanning ≥2 D-layers OR layer-unknown failures. How to choose mode: artifact at single layer → that topic skill's audit-mode; artifacts across layers OR layer unknown → `4dx-audit` cross-layer aggregator; vague intent / cold-start → `using-four-dx-coach` router. Multi-file skills self-detect scope (personal / team-leader / team-member) via internal Socratic disambiguation; single-file skills are scope-locked because the source book has no cross-scope variant. All skills industry-grounded — Boundary sections cite primary academic + regulatory + credentialed-author sources beyond the source book (Goodhart 1975 / Kotter 1996 / Tufte 1983 / Reinertsen 2009 / Doerr 2018 / Akao 1991 / Edmondson 2018 / Pfeffer 2010 / Drucker HBR 1999 / Senge 1990 / Lencioni 2002 / Pfeffer-Sutton 2006 / CFPB / VA OIG / etc.) — addresses book's selection-bias and member-POV gaps. Personal scope walks one user through full 4DX adoption. Team-leader scope covers Primary WIG selection, Targets-not-Plans cascade, leader-onboarding, lead-measure facilitation, scoreboard design, facilitator-mode WIG Session, and XPS audit. Team-member scope helps you comprehend an inherited team WIG, identify sphere of influence on inherited leads, read team scoreboard, prep weekly commitment, and self-account honestly. Distilled from McChesney/Covey/Huling/Thele/Walker, *The 4 Disciplines of Execution* (2nd ed., 2021) via tsundoku:book-distill RIA-TV++ pipeline; Plan U merge 2026-04-30 collapsed 26 skills → 11; v0.7.0 added consultant-mode entry point; v0.8.0 introduced dual-mode topic skills + repositioned `4dx-audit` as cross-layer aggregator. Multilingual triggers (EN / 日本語 / 繁體中文); skill bodies in English. NOT for enterprise multi-team rollout (read book Part 2 directly), habit formation (atomic habits), or portfolio betting (OKR / lean experiments).
